text:
There once was a small hamlet called Willow Creek.

In Willow Creek lived an old wood owl named Olive. Olive loved to fly around lazily at evenings. One particular evening she soared over fields lost in thought. Eventually she rested on a gnarled oak.

Something was wrong. Wind-whisper was howling. They drifted toward an old library. Leaves and twigs were fluttering like leaves. Something large could catch them.

They entered. A gentle old tome with delicate parchment. "That feather?" murmured Wisdom ears.

"A hawk..."

"Nothing that size," Wisdom frowned.

They flew out. A hawk with deep brown feathers.

"I'll get you," Olive whispered fiercely.

On took flight. They chased tirelessly. Just before Owl Eye saw night.

"Take this!"

Bird feathers?

"No." Olive laughed wildly.

The hawk soared. Olive caught a twig.

They flew back. Owl Eye was asleep. They snuck out quietly.

Next day Owls flew.

Olive? They
